category_title: Preschoolers who talk out of turn during group activities
student_description: Sybil is a highly verbal student who loves sharing stories but frequently talks over peers and adults without pausing or raising her hand. To successfully manage her turn-taking, she uniquely requires proactive visual reminders before activities and immediate positive reinforcement when she exhibits desired waiting behaviors.

criteria.rationale: Sybil needs visual reminders of expectations before an activity begins to help her regulate her talking and remember to raise her hand.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes the use of visual aids or picture reminders presented prior to group activities to cue hand-raising and turn-taking.

criteria.rationale: Sybil requires explicit teaching, modeling, and role-playing of turn-taking behaviors within the natural context of her daily routines.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates explicit teaching, modeling, or role-playing of listening and turn-taking skills during natural group settings (such as circle time).
